messages.content: Build a minimal Effect v3 program that can fail with two tagged errors (`NotFoundErr` and `AuthErr`) and use `Effect.catchTag` to recover from `NotFoundErr` only. Show the remaining error type in the final signature.

Build a small Effect v3 pipeline that fails with three tagged errors (`NetErr`, `ParseErr`, `AuthErr`) and uses `Effect.catchTags` to handle `NetErr` and `ParseErr` in a single call, leaving `AuthErr` on the error channel.
What's the Effect v3 way to collapse every error into a single fallback value? Show a minimal example using `Effect.catchAll`.
Explain briefly the difference between `Effect.catchAll`, `Effect.catchTag`, and `Effect.catchTags`, then show a working typed example that uses all three in the same pipeline.

Write an Effect v3 pipeline that fails with `DbErr | CacheErr | ValidationErr`. Constraints: recover `DbErr` and `CacheErr` via `Effect.catchTags`, leave `ValidationErr` on the error channel, no `any`.
Add a `catchAll` safety net to this Effect program so it can never fail. Snippet: `declare const run: Effect.Effect<User, NetErr | ParseErr>;`

Build an Effect v3 function that tries a primary source and falls back to a secondary source using `Effect.orElse`. Both sources can fail with the same tagged error; the fallback wins if the primary fails.
Build a small Effect v3 program that reads a config value, returning a default string when the read fails. Use `Effect.orElseSucceed` so the error channel becomes `never`.
What's the Effect v3 way to chain three fallbacks in order: A, then B, then C? Show a minimal example with `Effect.orElse`.
What's the difference between `Effect.orElse` and `Effect.orElseSucceed` in Effect v3? Show a minimal side-by-side example.

Write an Effect v3 loader that tries disk, then network, then a default. Constraints: use `Effect.orElse` for the first fallback, `Effect.orElseSucceed` for the final default, and return `Effect.Effect<Config, never>`.
Explain briefly why `Effect.orElse` is preferable to a manual `catchAll` when all you want is a fallback effect of the same shape, then show a typed example.
Build an Effect v3 tagged error `HttpErr` using `Data.TaggedError` with fields `status: number` and `url: string`. Provide a helper that fails with it, and a handler that reads both fields.
Build three tagged error classes with fields: `DbErr{query,code}`, `NetErr{url,status}`, `ValErr{field,hint}`. Each extends `Data.TaggedError`. Show a helper that fails with each one.

Write a tagged error `RateLimitErr` with fields `retryAfterMs: number` and `endpoint: string`. Constraints: export a `throw500` helper that fails with it and a handler that sleeps `retryAfterMs` before succeeding with `0`.

Add a `cause: unknown` field to this tagged error so callers can attach the original thrown value for logging. Snippet:

```ts
export class DecodeErr extends Data.TaggedError("DecodeErr")<{ readonly raw: string }> {}
```
What's the Effect v3 way to declare a tagged error that carries a nested object? Show a minimal example with a `headers` record field.
Build an Effect v3 helper that lifts an `Either<ParseErr, A>` into `Effect<A, ParseErr>` using `Effect.fromEither`. Include a tagged ParseErr.
Build an Effect v3 helper that turns a nullable lookup into an `Effect<A, NotFound>` using `Effect.fromNullable`. Include the NotFound tagged error and `Effect.mapError` to shape the error.
What's the Effect v3 way to turn an `Option<A>` into `Effect<A, NotFound>`? Show a minimal example using `Option.match` (the option-ts way).
What's the fp-ts way to convert an `Option<A>` into an `Either<E, A>` with a tagged error? Show a minimal example using `O.match`.
Write an Effect v3 function `parsePositive` that validates a string as a positive integer. Constraints: build the `Either` first, lift with `Effect.fromEither`, tagged errors only.
Explain briefly when to use `Effect.fromEither` vs `Effect.fromNullable` vs matching on an `Option`, then show a typed example that uses all three.
Build an Effect v3 retry policy that only retries on `NetErr`, not on `AuthErr`. Use `Schedule.recurs(3)` combined with `Schedule.whileInput` so the predicate decides whether to continue.
Build an Effect v3 fetcher that retries up to 5 times with a 100 ms exponential backoff but gives up on `ClientErr` (4xx). Use a `shouldContinue` predicate.
What's the Effect v3 way to retry only on transient errors and bail on permanent ones? Show a minimal example with `Effect.retry` + predicate.
Write an Effect v3 API call that retries on `NetErr` with exponential backoff + jitter, caps at 10 attempts, and never retries on `ParseErr`. Constraints: tagged errors, no `any`.

Add a retry-on-NetErr policy (3 attempts, 200ms between) to this Effect program. Snippet:

```ts
declare const call: Effect.Effect<Data, NetErr | AuthErr>;```
Explain briefly why you often want `Schedule.whileInput` when combining retries with a typed error union, then show a typed example.
Build an Effect v3 wrapper that calls a library returning `Effect<A, LibraryErr>` and re-maps the error to a domain `DomainErr` with `Effect.mapError`.
Build an fp-ts pipeline that parses a number from a string and uses `E.mapLeft` to turn a raw string error into a tagged `ParseErr`. Use `pipe` from `fp-ts/function`.
What's the Effect v3 way to collapse three different error tags into a single `AppErr` tag? Show a minimal example with `Effect.mapError`.

Refactor this Effect pipeline to use `Effect.mapError` instead of `Effect.catchAll`+rethrow for a simple tag rename. Snippet:

```ts
const out = call.pipe(Effect.catchAll((e) => Effect.fail(new WrapErr({ inner: e }))));```
Write an fp-ts `TaskEither` pipeline that calls `fetchJson` then `parseUser`, both can fail, mapping both left channels into a unified `AppErr` tag. Constraints: `TE.chain`, `TE.mapLeft`, no `any`.
Build an Effect v3 handler that exhaustively matches a three-tag error union (`TagA | TagB | TagC`). Use a `never` assertion in the default branch so adding a fourth tag becomes a compile error.

Build an Effect v3 program where the error type is a discriminated union of two sentinels `{ _tag: "NoResult" }` and `{ _tag: "Stale" }`. Match exhaustively with `Effect.matchTag`-style (but use `catchTags`).
What's the Effect v3 way to write an exhaustive error matcher that forces a compile error when a new tag is added? Show a minimal `_tag` switch example plugged into an `Effect` pipeline.

Fix this handler so it's exhaustive over the typed error union and the compiler catches a missing case. Snippet:

```ts
const f = (e: E1 | E2) => e._tag === "E1" ? "a" : "b";
```
Write an Effect v3 program for a four-tag error union using tagged errors as sentinels, and provide an exhaustive matcher. Constraints: `Effect.match`, `never` fallthrough, no `any`.
